title.en: Biographies of Wan Shi and Zhang Shu
title.raw: 《萬石張叔列傳》 Biographies of Wan Shi and Zhang Shu

sentences.zh: 萬石君名奮，其父趙人也，姓石氏。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wan Shi was named Fen, his father was from Zhao, surname Shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wanshi was named Fen. His father was a man of Zhao, surnamed Shi.

sentences.zh: 趙亡，徙居溫。

translations.literal: After Zhao perished, he moved to reside in Wen.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhao fell, he moved to Wen.

sentences.zh: 高祖東擊項籍，過河內，時奮年十五，為小吏，侍高祖。

translations.literal: When Gaozu struck east against Xiang Ji, passing through Henan, at that time Fen was fifteen years old, serving as a minor official attending Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: When Gaozu marched east to attack Xiang Ji and passed through Henei, Fen was fifteen years old, serving as a minor functionary in attendance on Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 高祖與語，愛其恭敬，問曰：「若何有？」

translations.literal: Gao Zu with speak, love his respectful reverence, ask said: 'You have what?'
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu spoke with him and loved his respectful reverence, asked: "What do you have?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「奮獨有母，不幸失明。

translations.literal: Answer said: 'Fen alone have mother, unfortunately lose sight.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Fen alone has a mother, unfortunately she has lost her sight.

sentences.zh: 家貧。

translations.literal: The family is poor.
translations.idiomatic: His family was poor.

sentences.zh: 有姊，能鼓琴。」

translations.literal: Have elder sister, able drum zither.'
translations.idiomatic: I have an elder sister who can play the zither."

sentences.zh: 高祖曰：「若能從我乎？」

translations.literal: Gao Zu said: 'You able follow me?'
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu said: "Can you follow me?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「願盡力。」

translations.literal: Said: 'Wish exhaust strength.'
translations.idiomatic: He said: "I wish to exhaust my strength."

sentences.zh: 於是高祖召其姊為美人，以奮為中涓，受書謁，徙其家長安中戚裏，以姊為美人故也。

translations.literal: Then Gaozu summoned his elder sister to become a beauty, made Fen a palace attendant receiving documents and audiences, moved his family to the Zhongqi quarter in Chang'an, because his elder sister became a beauty.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon Gaozu summoned his elder sister to serve as a court beauty, appointed Fen as a palace attendant in charge of receiving documents and audiences, and relocated his family to the Zhongqi quarter in Chang'an—all on account of his sister becoming a court beauty.

sentences.zh: 其官至孝文時，積功勞至大中大夫。

translations.literal: His official position reached during Emperor Wen's time, accumulating merit he reached Grand Palace Counselor.
translations.idiomatic: By the time of Emperor Wen, he had accumulated enough merit to rise to the rank of Grand Palace Counselor.

sentences.zh: 無文學，恭謹無與比。

translations.literal: Without literary learning, his respectful caution had no equal.
translations.idiomatic: He had no literary learning, but in respectful caution he was without equal.

translations.literal: Wan Shi Jun named Fen, his father Zhao person, surname Shi clan. Zhao perished, moved dwell Wen. Gao Zu east strike Xiang Ji, pass He Nei, at time Fen year fifteen, become small li, serve Gao Zu. Gao Zu with speak, love his respectful reverence, ask said: 'You have what?' Answer said: 'Fen alone have mother, unfortunately lose sight. Family poor. Have elder sister, able drum zither.' Gao Zu said: 'You able follow me?' Said: 'Wish exhaust strength.' Then Gao Zu summon his elder sister become beauty, with Fen become zhong jian, receive book ye, move his family Chang An Zhong Qi Li, with elder sister become beauty reason. His official reach Xiao Wen time, accumulate merit labor reach da zhong da fu. No literature, respectful cautious no equal compare.

sentences.zh: 文帝時，東陽侯張相如為太子太傅，免。

translations.literal: During Emperor Wen's time, the Marquis of Dongyang Zhang Xiangru served as crown prince's Grand Tutor, was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: During Emperor Wen's reign, the Marquis of Dongyang, Zhang Xiangru, served as Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince but was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 選可為傅者，皆推奮，奮為太子太傅。

translations.literal: When selecting someone suitable as tutor, all recommended Fen, so Fen became crown prince's Grand Tutor.
translations.idiomatic: When a suitable replacement was being selected, all recommended Fen, and so Fen became Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 及孝景即位，以為九卿；

translations.literal: Reach Xiao Jing establish, become nine qing;
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing ascended, he became one of the Nine Ministers;

sentences.zh: 迫近，憚之，徙奮為諸侯相。

translations.literal: Pressing close, they feared him, transferred Fen to become chancellor of feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Being too close to the throne, others feared him, and so Fen was transferred to serve as chancellor of a feudal kingdom.

sentences.zh: 奮長子建，次子甲，次子乙，次子慶，皆以馴行孝謹，官皆至二千石。

translations.literal: Fen's eldest son Jian, second son Jia, third son Yi, fourth son Qing, all through their tame conduct filial piety and caution, their official positions all reached two thousand dan.
translations.idiomatic: Fen's eldest son Jian, second son Jia, third son Yi, and fourth son Qing all attained the rank of two-thousand-bushel official through their disciplined conduct, filial piety, and caution.

sentences.zh: 於是景帝曰：「石君及四子皆二千石，人臣尊寵乃集其門。」

translations.literal: Then Jing Di said: 'Shi Jun and four sons all two thousand dan, person minister honor favor then collect his gate.'
translations.idiomatic: Then Emperor Jing said: "The Marquis of Shi and his four sons are all two thousand dan—ministerial honor and favor all gather at his gate."

sentences.zh: 號奮為萬石君。

translations.literal: He titled Fen the Marquis of Wan Shi.
translations.idiomatic: He bestowed upon Fen the title Marquis of Wanshi.

translations.literal: Wen Di time, Dong Yang Hou Zhang Xiang Ru become crown prince tai fu, free. Select able become fu, all push Fen, Fen become crown prince tai fu. Reach Xiao Jing establish, become nine qing; Press close, fear him, move Fen become feudal lord xiang. Fen long son Jian, next son Jia, next son Yi, next son Qing, all with tame conduct filial cautious, officials all reach two thousand dan. Then Jing Di said: 'Shi Jun and four sons all two thousand dan, person minister honor favor then collect his gate.' Number Fen become Wan Shi Jun.

sentences.zh: 孝景帝季年，萬石君以上大夫祿歸老于家，以歲時為朝臣。

translations.literal: In the later years of Emperor Jing, the Marquis of Wan Shi returned home to retire on the salary of Grand Palace Counselor, serving as court minister by season.
translations.idiomatic: In the later years of Emperor Jing's reign, the Marquis of Wanshi retired to his home on the salary of a Senior Grand Counselor, attending court only at seasonal assemblies.

sentences.zh: 過宮門闕，萬石君必下車趨，見路馬必式焉。

translations.literal: Passing the palace gate towers, the Marquis of Wan Shi would certainly descend his chariot and hasten, seeing road horses he would certainly salute.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he passed the gate-towers of the palace, the Marquis of Wanshi would invariably step down from his carriage and walk briskly past. Whenever he saw the imperial carriage horses on the road, he would bow in salute.

sentences.zh: 子孫為小吏，來歸謁，萬石君必朝服見之，不名。

translations.literal: When sons and grandsons serving as minor officials came home to visit, the Marquis of Wan Shi would certainly wear court robes to see them, not using names.
translations.idiomatic: When his sons and grandsons who served as minor officials came home to pay their respects, the Marquis of Wanshi would invariably receive them in full court dress, never addressing them by name.

sentences.zh: 子孫有過失，不譙讓，為便坐，對案不食。

translations.literal: When sons and grandsons had faults and errors, he would not scold or blame them, but would make them sit conveniently, facing the table not eating.
translations.idiomatic: When his sons and grandsons committed errors, he would not scold or reproach them but would simply sit before the table and refuse to eat.

sentences.zh: 然後諸子相責，因長老肉袒固謝罪，改之，乃許。

translations.literal: Then the various sons would mutually reproach each other, because of the elders stripping to the waist and firmly apologizing for the offense, changing it, then he would allow it.
translations.idiomatic: Then the sons would reproach one another, and the elders would bare their shoulders and earnestly beg forgiveness. Only when the offender had reformed would he relent.

sentences.zh: 子孫勝冠者在側，雖燕居必冠，申申如也。

translations.literal: When sons and grandsons who had capped were at his side, though in private residence he would certainly wear caps, Shen Shen as it should be.
translations.idiomatic: When sons and grandsons who had undergone the capping ceremony were at his side, he would always wear his cap even in private, bearing himself with grave composure.

sentences.zh: 僮仆訢訢如也，唯謹。

translations.literal: The servants and attendants Xin Xin as it should be, only cautious.
translations.idiomatic: His servants and attendants went about cheerfully, all with the utmost caution.

sentences.zh: 上時賜食於家，必稽首俯伏而食之，如在上前。

translations.literal: When the emperor occasionally granted food at home, he would certainly bow his head and prostrate to eat it, as if before the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever the emperor bestowed food upon his household, he would invariably bow his head to the ground and prostrate himself before eating it, as though he were in the emperor's presence.

sentences.zh: 其執喪，哀戚甚悼。

translations.literal: Their observance of mourning was sorrowfully sad and deeply mournful.
translations.idiomatic: When the family observed mourning, their grief was profound and deeply sorrowful.

sentences.zh: 子孫遵教，亦如之。

translations.literal: Sons and grandsons followed the teachings, also like this.
translations.idiomatic: His sons and grandsons followed his teachings and conducted themselves in the same manner.

sentences.zh: 萬石君家以孝謹聞乎郡國，雖齊魯諸儒質行，皆自以為不及也。

translations.literal: The Wan Shi Jun household was known for filial piety and caution throughout commanderies and kingdoms, though the various Confucian scholars of Qi and Lu in solid conduct, all considered themselves not equal.
translations.idiomatic: The household of the Marquis of Wanshi was renowned for filial piety and caution throughout the commanderies and kingdoms. Even the Confucian scholars of Qi and Lu, known for their upright conduct, all considered themselves his inferiors.

translations.literal: Xiao Jing Di last years, Wan Shi Jun with shang da fu lu return old at home, with year season become court minister. Pass palace gate que, Wan Shi Jun must descend chariot hasten, see road horse must shi. Son grandson become small li, come return ye, Wan Shi Jun must court robe see them, not name. Son grandson have fault error, not scold blame, make convenient seat, face table not eat. Then various sons mutual blame, because elder strip flesh solid thank crime, change it, then allow. Son grandson win crown at side, though Yan dwell must crown, Shen Shen like. Tong Pu Xin Xin like, only cautious. Shang time grant food at home, must bow head bow prostrate eat it, like at shang front. Their hold death, sorrow sad very mourn. Son grandson follow teach, also like it. Wan Shi Jun home with filial cautious hear at jun state, though Qi Lu various ru solid conduct, all self consider not reach.

sentences.zh: 建元二年，郎中令王臧以文學獲罪。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianyuan, the Palace Steward Wang Zang was convicted for literary matters.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of the Jianyuan era, the Palace Steward Wang Zang was convicted on account of his literary activities.

sentences.zh: 皇太后以為儒者文多質少，今萬石君家不言而躬行，乃以長子建為郎中令，少子慶為內史。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager considered scholars have much text but little substance, now the Wan Shi Jun household does not speak but practices with their bodies, so she made the eldest son Jian Palace Steward, the youngest son Qing Interior Minister.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager considered that Confucian scholars were long on rhetoric but short on substance, whereas the household of the Marquis of Wanshi spoke little but practiced virtue in person. She therefore appointed the eldest son Jian as Palace Steward and the youngest son Qing as Interior Minister.

translations.literal: Jian Yuan two years, lang zhong ling Wang Zang with literature obtain crime. Huang tai hou with ru scholar text many quality few, now Wan Shi Jun home not speak yet body walk, then with long son Jian become lang zhong ling, young son Qing become nei shi.

sentences.zh: 建老白首，萬石君尚無恙。

translations.literal: Jian grew old with white hair, the Marquis of Wan Shi was still without illness.
translations.idiomatic: By the time Jian had grown old and white-haired, the Marquis of Wanshi was still in good health.

sentences.zh: 建為郎中令，每五日洗沐歸謁親，入子舍，竊問侍者，取親中帬廁牏，身自浣滌，復與侍者，不敢令萬石君知，以為常。

translations.literal: Jian served as Palace Steward, every five days he would wash and bathe then return home to visit his parents, entering the children's quarters, secretly asking the servants, taking the parents' jackets and toilet mats, personally washing and cleaning them, then returning them to the servants, not daring to let the Marquis of Wan Shi know, making it his constant practice.
translations.idiomatic: While serving as Palace Steward, Jian would return home every five days during his rest period to visit his parents. He would slip into the servants' quarters, secretly ask for his father's undergarments and chamber-pot linings, wash and clean them himself, then return them to the servants—never daring to let the Marquis of Wanshi know. This became his constant practice.

sentences.zh: 建為郎中令，事有可言，屏人恣言，極切；

translations.literal: Jian become lang zhong ling, affairs have able speak, screen person unrestrained speak, extreme cut;
translations.idiomatic: Jian served as Palace Steward, when affairs could be spoken of, he would screen people and speak unrestrainedly, extremely cutting;

sentences.zh: 至廷見，如不能言者。

translations.literal: When reaching court audiences, he was like one unable to speak.
translations.idiomatic: Yet at court audiences, he appeared as though he could not speak a word.

sentences.zh: 是以上乃親尊禮之。

translations.literal: Therefore the emperor personally honored and ritualized him.
translations.idiomatic: For this reason, the emperor personally honored him and treated him with the highest respect.

translations.literal: Jian old white head, Wan Shi Jun still no illness. Jian become lang zhong ling, every five days wash bathe return ye close, enter son shed, secretly ask serve, take close middle jacket toilet mat, body self wash clean, again with serve, not dare make Wan Shi Jun know, become constant. Jian become lang zhong ling, affairs have able speak, screen person unrestrained speak, extreme cut; Reach court see, like cannot speak. Therefore shang then close honor ritual him.

sentences.zh: 萬石君徙居陵裏。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wan Shi moved to reside in Ling quarter.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wanshi moved his residence to the Ling quarter.

sentences.zh: 內史慶醉歸，入外門不下車。

translations.literal: The Interior Minister Qing returned drunk, entering the outer gate without descending his chariot.
translations.idiomatic: The Interior Minister Qing returned home drunk and entered the outer gate without descending from his carriage.

sentences.zh: 萬石君聞之，不食。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wan Shi heard of it and would not eat.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Wanshi heard of this, he refused to eat.

sentences.zh: 慶恐，肉袒請罪，不許。

translations.literal: Qing feared, stripped to the waist and apologized for the offense, but was not allowed.
translations.idiomatic: Qing was frightened and bared his shoulders to beg forgiveness, but the Marquis would not accept.

sentences.zh: 舉宗及兄建肉袒，萬石君讓曰：「內史貴人，入閭里，里中長老皆走匿，而內史坐車中自如，固當！」

translations.literal: Raise clan and brother Jian strip flesh, Wan Shi Jun yield said: 'Nei shi noble person, enter li li, li middle elder all walk hide, yet nei shi sit chariot middle self like, certainly should!'
translations.idiomatic: The entire clan and brother Jian stripped to the waist, the Marquis of Wan Shi yielded saying: "The Interior Minister is a noble person, entering the alley quarters, the elders of the quarter all walk and hide, yet the Interior Minister sits in his chariot as usual, certainly should!"

sentences.zh: 乃謝罷慶。

translations.literal: Then he thanked and dismissed Qing.
translations.idiomatic: Only then did he forgive and dismiss Qing.

sentences.zh: 慶及諸子弟入里門，趨至家。

translations.literal: Qing and the various sons and younger brothers entered the quarter gate, hastening home.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, Qing and all the sons and younger brothers would enter the quarter gate and hasten home on foot.

translations.literal: Wan Shi Jun move dwell Ling Li. Nei shi Qing drunk return, enter outer gate not descend chariot. Wan Shi Jun hear, not eat. Qing fear, strip flesh please crime, not allow. Raise clan and brother Jian strip flesh, Wan Shi Jun yield said: 'Nei shi noble person, enter li li, li middle elder all walk hide, yet nei shi sit chariot middle self like, certainly should!' Then thank stop Qing. Qing and various sons brothers enter li gate, hasten reach home.

sentences.zh: 萬石君以元朔五年中卒。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wan Shi died in the middle of the fifth year of Yuanshuo.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wanshi died in the fifth year of the Yuanshuo era.

sentences.zh: 長子郎中令建哭泣哀思，扶杖乃能行。

translations.literal: The eldest son Palace Steward Jian wept and mourned sorrowfully, supporting a staff he could then walk.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son, the Palace Steward Jian, wept so piteously in his grief that he could walk only by leaning on a staff.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，建亦死。

translations.literal: Over a year later, Jian also died.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a year later, Jian too died.

sentences.zh: 諸子孫咸孝，然建最甚，甚於萬石君。

translations.literal: All the sons and grandsons were filial, but Jian was most extreme, even more than the Marquis of Wan Shi.
translations.idiomatic: All the sons and grandsons were filial, but Jian was the most extreme of all—surpassing even the Marquis of Wanshi himself.

translations.literal: Wan Shi Jun with Yuan Shuo five years middle die. Long son lang zhong ling Jian cry weep sorrow think, support staff then able walk. Year remain, Jian also die. Various sons grandson all filial, yet Jian most extreme, extreme surpass Wan Shi Jun.

sentences.zh: 建為郎中令，書奏事，事下，建讀之，曰：「誤書！

translations.literal: Jian become lang zhong ling, book memorial affairs, affairs descend, Jian read it, said: 'Mistake book!
translations.idiomatic: Jian served as Palace Steward, when book memorials on affairs were submitted, affairs descended, Jian read them and said: "Mistake in the book!

sentences.zh: 『馬』者與尾當五，今乃四，不足一。

translations.literal: 'Horse' with tail should five, now then four, not enough one.
translations.idiomatic: "Horse" with tail should be five, now it is four, lacking one."

sentences.zh: 上譴死矣！」

translations.literal: Shang blame die!'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor will blame me to death!"

sentences.zh: 甚惶恐。

translations.literal: He was greatly panicked and fearful.
translations.idiomatic: He was greatly alarmed and fearful.

sentences.zh: 其為謹慎，雖他皆如是。

translations.literal: His being cautious and careful, though others were all like this.
translations.idiomatic: Such was his caution—and in all other matters he was just the same.

translations.literal: Jian become lang zhong ling, book memorial affairs, affairs descend, Jian read it, said: 'Mistake book! 'Horse' with tail should five, now then four, not enough one. Shang blame die!' Very panic fear. His become cautious careful, though other all like this.

sentences.zh: 萬石君少子慶為太仆，御出，上問車中幾馬，慶以策數馬畢，舉手曰：「六馬。」

translations.literal: Wan Shi Jun young son Qing become tai pu, yu out, shang ask chariot middle several horse, Qing with whip count horse finish, raise hand said: 'Six horse.'
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wan Shi's youngest son Qing served as Grand Coachman, driving out, the emperor asked how many horses were in the chariot, Qing counted the horses with his whip, raised his hand and said: "Six horses."

sentences.zh: 慶於諸子中最為簡易矣，然猶如此。

translations.literal: Qing among the various sons was the most simple and easy, yet still like this.
translations.idiomatic: Qing was the most casual and easygoing among the sons, yet even he behaved this way.

sentences.zh: 為齊相，舉齊國皆慕其家行，不言而齊國大治，為立石相祠。

translations.literal: Serving as Qi chancellor, he raised Qi state all admiring his family conduct, not speaking yet Qi state greatly governed, they established a stone chancellor temple for him.
translations.idiomatic: When he served as Chancellor of Qi, the entire kingdom admired his family's conduct. Without a word of command, Qi was brought to excellent order, and the people erected a stone shrine to Chancellor Shi in his honor.

translations.literal: Wan Shi Jun young son Qing become tai pu, yu out, shang ask chariot middle several horse, Qing with whip count horse finish, raise hand said: 'Six horse.' Qing at various sons middle most become simple easy, yet still like this. Become Qi xiang, raise Qi state all admire his home conduct, not speak yet Qi state great govern, become establish stone xiang temple.

sentences.zh: 元狩元年，上立太子，選群臣可為傅者，慶自沛守為太子太傅，七歲遷為御史大夫。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanshou, the emperor established the crown prince, selecting ministers suitable as tutors, Qing from Pei governor became crown prince's Grand Tutor, after seven years was promoted to Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Yuanshou era, the emperor established the Crown Prince and selected from among his ministers those fit to serve as tutor. Qing was transferred from his post as Governor of Pei to become Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince, and after seven years was promoted to Imperial Secretary.

translations.literal: Yuan Shou one year, shang establish crown prince, select group minister able become fu, Qing self Pei guard become crown prince tai fu, seven years move become yu shi da fu.

sentences.zh: 元鼎五年秋，丞相有罪，罷。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the fifth year of Yuanding, the Chancellor had crime, was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the fifth year of the Yuanding era, the Chancellor was found guilty and dismissed.

sentences.zh: 制詔御史：「萬石君先帝尊之，子孫孝，其以御史大夫慶為丞相，封為牧丘侯。」

translations.literal: System zhao yu shi: 'Wan Shi Jun former di honor him, sons grandson filial, its with yu shi da fu Qing become cheng xiang, seal become Mu Qiu hou.'
translations.idiomatic: Imperial edict to the Imperial Secretary: "The Marquis of Wan Shi was honored by the former emperor, his sons and grandsons are filial, therefore appoint the Imperial Secretary Qing as Chancellor, seal him as Marquis of Muqiu."

sentences.zh: 是時漢方南誅兩越，東擊朝鮮，北逐匈奴，西伐大宛，中國多事。

translations.literal: At this time Han was just punishing the two Yue in the south, striking Chaoxian in the east, pursuing the Xiongnu in the north, attacking Dayuan in the west, China had many affairs.
translations.idiomatic: At this time, the Han was campaigning against the two Yue kingdoms in the south, striking Chaoxian in the east, pursuing the Xiongnu in the north, and attacking Dayuan in the west. The empire was beset with affairs.

sentences.zh: 天子巡狩海內，修上古神祠，封禪，興禮樂。

translations.literal: The emperor toured and hunted within the seas, repaired the ancient god temples, performed the Feng and Shan sacrifices, raised rites and music.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor toured and hunted throughout the realm, restored the ancient divine shrines, performed the Feng and Shan sacrifices, and revived the rites and music.

sentences.zh: 公家用少，桑弘羊等致利，王溫舒之屬峻法，兒寬等推文學至九卿，更進用事，事不關決於丞相，丞相醇謹而已。

translations.literal: Public family expenditures were few, Sang Hongyang and others achieved profits, Wang Wenshu and others enforced severe laws, Er Kuan and others promoted literature to reach the Nine Ministers, changing to advance those who handled affairs, affairs not closely decided by the Chancellor, the Chancellor was pure and cautious only.
translations.idiomatic: The state coffers were running low. Sang Hongyang and his like raised revenue, Wang Wenshu and his sort enforced harsh laws, and Er Kuan and others advanced through scholarship to the rank of the Nine Ministers. Those who handled affairs were the ones promoted, and decisions were no longer referred to the Chancellor. The Chancellor was simply upright and cautious, nothing more.

sentences.zh: 在位九歲，無能有所匡言。

translations.literal: In position nine years, unable to have any rectifying words.
translations.idiomatic: In nine years in office, he was never able to offer a single word of correction.

sentences.zh: 嘗欲請治上近臣所忠、九卿咸宣罪，不能服，反受其過，贖罪。

translations.literal: Once wished to request governing the emperor's close ministers Su Zhong, the Nine Ministers all publicly announcing crimes, could not convince, instead received their blame, redeemed the crime.
translations.idiomatic: He once tried to request the prosecution of the emperor's close minister Suo Zhong and to publicly declare the crimes of the Nine Ministers, but he could not prevail. Instead, he himself received the blame and had to ransom his offense.

translations.literal: Yuan Ding five years autumn, cheng xiang have crime, stop. System zhao yu shi: 'Wan Shi Jun former di honor him, sons grandson filial, its with yu shi da fu Qing become cheng xiang, seal become Mu Qiu hou.' This time Han just south punish two Yue, east strike Chao Xian, north pursue Xiong Nu, west attack Da Wan, Zhong Guo many affairs. Tian zi patrol hunt sea within, repair shang ancient god temple, seal zhan, rise li music. Public family use few, Sang Hong Yang wait achieve profit, Wang Wen Shu wait severe law, Er Kuan wait push literature reach nine qing, change advance use affairs, affairs not close decide at cheng xiang, cheng xiang pure cautious only. In position nine years, no able have rectify speak. Once desire please govern shang close minister Su Zhong, nine qing all Xuan crime, cannot serve, instead receive their fault, redeem crime.

sentences.zh: 元封四年中，關東流民二百萬口，無名數者四十萬，公卿議欲請徙流民於邊以適之。

translations.literal: In the middle of the fourth year of Yuanfeng, Guandong flowing people two hundred thousand mouths, nameless and unnumbered forty thousand, the ministers discussed desiring to request moving the flowing people to the border to adapt them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of the Yuanfeng era, there were two million displaced refugees east of the passes, with another four hundred thousand unregistered. The ministers debated whether to petition for the refugees to be relocated to the frontier.

sentences.zh: 上以為丞相老謹，不能與其議，乃賜丞相告歸，而案御史大夫以下議為請者。

translations.literal: The emperor considered the Chancellor old and cautious, could not discuss with him, so granted the Chancellor to announce his retirement, and investigated the Imperial Secretary and below who had discussed and requested this.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor considered the Chancellor old and overly cautious, unable to take part in such deliberations, and so granted the Chancellor leave to retire. He then investigated the Imperial Secretary and those below who had proposed the petition.

sentences.zh: 丞相慚不任職，乃上書曰：「慶幸得待罪丞相，罷駑無以輔治，城郭倉庫空虛，民多流亡，罪當伏斧質，上不忍致法。

translations.literal: Cheng xiang shame not bear duty, then shang book said: 'Qing fortunate obtain wait crime cheng xiang, dismiss nu no with auxiliary govern, city wall warehouse empty virtual, people many flow perish, crime should prostrate axe block, shang not bear apply law.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor was ashamed not to bear his duty, so submitted a memorial saying: "Qing fortunately obtained waiting in crime as Chancellor, dismissed and incapable of assisting governance, city walls and warehouses empty and void, people many flowing and perishing, crime should prostrate at axe and block, Your Majesty not bearing to apply the law.

sentences.zh: 願歸丞相侯印，乞骸骨歸，避賢者路。」

translations.literal: Wish return cheng xiang hou seal, beg skeleton return, avoid worthy road.'
translations.idiomatic: I wish to return the Chancellor and Marquis seals, beg to return my bones, avoid the worthy one's path."

sentences.zh: 天子曰：「倉廩既空，民貧流亡，而君欲請徙之，搖蕩不安，動危之，而辭位，君欲安歸難乎？」

translations.literal: Tian zi said: 'Warehouse granary already empty, people poor flow perish, yet jun desire please move them, shake dang not settle, move danger it, yet resign position, jun desire settle return difficulty?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "Granaries already empty, people poor and flowing away, yet you desire to request moving them, shaking and unsettling, moving to endanger them, yet resign your position—where do you desire to settle and return to difficulty?"

sentences.zh: 以書讓慶，慶甚慚，遂復視事。

translations.literal: He yielded to Qing with a letter, Qing was very ashamed, then again oversaw affairs.
translations.idiomatic: He rebuked Qing by letter. Qing was deeply ashamed and thereupon resumed his duties.

translations.literal: Yuan Feng four years middle, Guan Dong flow people two hundred wan mouth, no name number forty wan, gong qing discuss desire please move flow people at border with adapt it. Shang with cheng xiang old cautious, cannot with his discuss, then grant cheng xiang tell return, yet examine yu shi da fu below discuss become please. Cheng xiang shame not bear duty, then shang book said: 'Qing fortunate obtain wait crime cheng xiang, dismiss nu no with auxiliary govern, city wall warehouse empty virtual, people many flow perish, crime should prostrate axe block, shang not bear apply law. Wish return cheng xiang hou seal, beg skeleton return, avoid worthy road.' Tian zi said: 'Warehouse granary already empty, people poor flow perish, yet jun desire please move them, shake dang not settle, move danger it, yet resign position, jun desire settle return difficulty?' With book yield Qing, Qing very shame, then again see affairs.

sentences.zh: 慶文深審謹，然無他大略，為百姓言。

translations.literal: Qing was cultured deeply examining and cautious, yet had no other great strategies, spoke for the people.
translations.idiomatic: Qing was learned, thorough, and cautious, yet he had no grand strategies to offer and spoke only on behalf of the common people.

sentences.zh: 後三歲餘，太初二年中，丞相慶卒，謚為恬侯。

translations.literal: After three years remaining, in the middle of the second year of Taichu, Chancellor Qing died, posthumous title Marquis Tian.
translations.idiomatic: A little over three years later, in the second year of the Taichu era, Chancellor Qing died and was posthumously titled Marquis Tian.

sentences.zh: 慶中子德，慶愛用之，上以德為嗣，代侯。

translations.literal: Qing's middle son De, Qing loved and used him, the emperor made De his successor, replacing as Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Qing's second son De was beloved and favored by Qing. The emperor appointed De as his heir to succeed as marquis.

sentences.zh: 後為太常，坐法當死，贖免為庶人。

translations.literal: Afterward became Grand Master of Ceremonies, convicted by law should die, redeemed and freed to become commoner.
translations.idiomatic: He later served as Grand Master of Ceremonies but was convicted of a capital offense. He ransomed himself and was reduced to a commoner.

sentences.zh: 慶方為丞相，諸子孫為吏更至二千石者十三人。

translations.literal: When Qing just became Chancellor, his various sons and grandsons who became officials reaching two thousand dan were thirteen people.
translations.idiomatic: When Qing first became Chancellor, thirteen of his sons and grandsons had attained the rank of two-thousand-bushel officials.

sentences.zh: 及慶死後，稍以罪去，孝謹益衰矣。

translations.literal: When Qing died, gradually they left due to crimes, filial caution increasingly declined.
translations.idiomatic: After Qing's death, they gradually fell from office due to offenses, and the family's tradition of filial caution steadily declined.

translations.literal: Qing culture deep examine cautious, yet no other great strategy, become people speak. After three years remain, Tai Chu two years middle, cheng xiang Qing die, shou become Tian hou. Qing middle son De, Qing love use him, shang with De become continue, replace hou. After become tai chang, sit law should die, redeem free become common person. Qing just become cheng xiang, various sons grandson become li change reach two thousand dan thirteen people. Reach Qing die after, gradually with crime go, filial cautious benefit decline.

sentences.zh: 建陵侯衛綰者，代大陵人也。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Jianling Wei Wan was a person from Daliang in Dai.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Jianling, Wei Wan, was a man of Daling in Dai.

sentences.zh: 綰以戲車為郎，事文帝，功次遷為中郎將，醇謹無他。

translations.literal: Wan became a mounted attendant through chariot games, served Emperor Wen, merit successively promoted to Colonel, pure and cautious without other qualities.
translations.idiomatic: Wan entered service as a mounted attendant through his skill in chariot games, serving Emperor Wen. Through accumulated merit he was successively promoted to Colonel of the Palace Guard—upright and cautious, with no other notable qualities.

sentences.zh: 孝景為太子時，召上左右飲，而綰稱病不行。

translations.literal: When Emperor Jing was crown prince, summoned the emperor's left and right to drink, but Wan claimed illness and did not go.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing was still Crown Prince, he summoned the emperor's attendants to drink with him, but Wan pleaded illness and did not go.

sentences.zh: 文帝且崩時，屬孝景曰：「綰長者，善遇之。」

translations.literal: Wen Di near collapse time, belong Xiao Jing said: 'Wan long person, good treat him.'
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen was about to pass away, entrusted Emperor Jing saying: "Wan is a venerable elder, treat him well."

sentences.zh: 及文帝崩，景帝立，歲餘不噍呵綰，綰日以謹力。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen passed away and Emperor Jing ascended, over a year did not scold or berate Wan, Wan daily exerted himself cautiously.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Wen died and Emperor Jing ascended the throne, more than a year passed without his ever scolding or rebuking Wan. Wan redoubled his cautious diligence each day.

translations.literal: Jian Ling hou Wei Wan, Dai Da Ling person. Wan with xi che become lang, serve Wen Di, merit next move become zhong lang jiang, pure cautious no other. Xiao Jing become crown prince time, summon shang left right drink, yet Wan claim illness not go. Wen Di near collapse time, belong Xiao Jing said: 'Wan long person, good treat him.' Reach Wen Di collapse, Jing Di establish, year remain not scold he Wan, Wan day with cautious strength.

sentences.zh: 景帝幸上林，詔中郎將參乘，還而問曰：「君知所以得參乘乎？」

translations.literal: Jing Di fortunate Shang Lin, zhao zhong lang jiang can ride, return yet ask said: 'Jun know so obtain can ride?'
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing visited Shanglin, commanded the Colonel to ride companion, returning asked: "Do you know why you obtained to ride companion?"

sentences.zh: 綰曰：「臣從車士幸得以功次遷為中郎將，不自知也。」

translations.literal: Wan said: 'Chen follow che shi fortunate able with merit next move become zhong lang jiang, not self know.'
translations.idiomatic: Wan said: "I followed as chariot warrior fortunate to be promoted to Colonel through merit, I do not know myself."

sentences.zh: 上問曰：「吾為太子時召君，君不肯來，何也？」

translations.literal: Shang ask said: 'I become crown prince time summon jun, jun not willing come, what?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked: "When I was crown prince I summoned you, you were unwilling to come, why?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「死罪，實病！」

translations.literal: Answer said: 'Die crime, real illness!'
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Capital crime, really ill!"

sentences.zh: 上賜之劍。

translations.literal: The emperor granted him a sword.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor bestowed a sword upon him.

sentences.zh: 綰曰：「先帝賜臣劍凡六，劍不敢奉詔。」

translations.literal: Wan said: 'Former di grant chen sword altogether six, sword not dare receive zhao.'
translations.idiomatic: Wan said: "The former emperor granted me swords altogether six, I dare not receive the command."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「劍，人之所施易，獨至今乎？」

translations.literal: Shang said: 'Sword, person place apply easy, alone reach now?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "Swords are what people use easily, only until now?"

sentences.zh: 綰曰：「具在。」

translations.literal: Wan said: 'All exist.'
translations.idiomatic: Wan said: "All exist."

sentences.zh: 上使取六劍，劍尚盛，未嘗服也。

translations.literal: The emperor had the six swords taken, the swords were still abundant, he had never used them.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had the six swords brought out. They were still in pristine condition—he had never once worn them.

sentences.zh: 郎官有譴，常蒙其罪，不與他將爭；

translations.literal: Lang official have blame, constant cover his crime, not with other jiang contend;
translations.idiomatic: When mounted officials had blame, he constantly covered their crimes, did not contend with other generals;

sentences.zh: 有功，常讓他將。

translations.literal: When there was merit, he constantly yielded to other generals.
translations.idiomatic: When there was merit to be claimed, he always deferred to the other generals.

sentences.zh: 上以為廉，忠實無他腸，乃拜綰為河閒王太傅。

translations.literal: The emperor considered him clean, loyal and real without other intentions, then appointed Wan as Hejian King's Grand Tutor.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor considered him incorruptible, loyal, and sincere, without any ulterior motives, and so appointed Wan as Grand Tutor to the King of Hejian.

sentences.zh: 吳楚反，詔綰為將，將河閒兵擊吳楚有功，拜為中尉。

translations.literal: Wu and Chu rebelled, commanded Wan to become general, led Hejian troops struck Wu and Chu with merit, appointed as Colonel.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu and Chu rebelled, an edict appointed Wan as general. He led the troops of Hejian against Wu and Chu with distinction, and was appointed Commandant.

sentences.zh: 三歲，以軍功，孝景前六年中封綰為建陵侯。

translations.literal: Three years, through army merit, in the middle of the sixth year of Emperor Jing's reign before his death, sealed Wan as Marquis of Jianling.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, on account of his military merit, in the sixth year of Emperor Jing's first reign period, Wan was enfeoffed as Marquis of Jianling.

translations.literal: Jing Di fortunate Shang Lin, zhao zhong lang jiang can ride, return yet ask said: 'Jun know so obtain can ride?' Wan said: 'Chen follow che shi fortunate able with merit next move become zhong lang jiang, not self know.' Shang ask said: 'I become crown prince time summon jun, jun not willing come, what?' Answer said: 'Die crime, real illness!' Shang grant him sword. Wan said: 'Former di grant chen sword altogether six, sword not dare receive zhao.' Shang said: 'Sword, person place apply easy, alone reach now?' Wan said: 'All exist.' Shang make take six sword, sword still abundant, not once use. Lang official have blame, constant cover his crime, not with other jiang contend; Have merit, constant yield other jiang. Shang with him clean, loyal real no other intestine, then appoint Wan become He Jian wang tai fu. Wu Chu rebel, zhao Wan become jiang, lead He Jian troops strike Wu Chu have merit, appoint become zhong wei. Three years, with army merit, Xiao Jing former six years middle seal Wan become Jian Ling hou.

sentences.zh: 其明年，上廢太子，誅栗卿之屬。

translations.literal: The next year, the emperor deposed the crown prince, executed Li Qing and his group.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, the emperor deposed the Crown Prince and executed Li Qing and his associates.

sentences.zh: 上以為綰長者，不忍，乃賜綰告歸，而使郅都治捕栗氏。

translations.literal: The emperor considered Wan a venerable elder, could not bear it, so granted Wan to announce retirement, and had Zhi Du govern and catch the Li clan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor regarded Wan as a venerable elder who could not bear such matters, and so granted Wan leave to retire. He then dispatched Zhi Du to arrest the Li clan.

sentences.zh: 既已，上立膠東王為太子，召綰，拜為太子太傅。

translations.literal: Afterward, the emperor established the King of Jiaodong as crown prince, summoned Wan, appointed him crown prince's Grand Tutor.
translations.idiomatic: When this was done, the emperor established the King of Jiaodong as Crown Prince, summoned Wan, and appointed him Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 久之，遷為御史大夫。

translations.literal: After a long time, promoted to Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: After a long while, he was promoted to Imperial Secretary.

sentences.zh: 五歲，代桃侯舍為丞相，朝奏事如職所奏。

translations.literal: Seven years, Emperor Wu ascended, appointed Wan as Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: After five years, he replaced the Marquis of Tao, She, as Chancellor, presenting memorials to the court as the duties of his office required.

sentences.zh: 然自初官以至丞相，終無可言。

translations.literal: Yet from beginning as official until Chancellor, ultimately had nothing to say.
translations.idiomatic: Yet from his first appointment as an official all the way to the chancellorship, he ultimately had nothing worth saying.

sentences.zh: 天子以為敦厚，可相少主，尊寵之，賞賜甚多。

translations.literal: The emperor considered him sincere and substantial, able to be Chancellor for the young ruler, honored and favored him, rewards and grants very many.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor considered him sincere and steadfast, fit to serve as Chancellor for a young ruler, and honored and favored him greatly, bestowing many rewards and gifts.

translations.literal: Its next year, shang waste crown prince, kill Li Qing wait. Shang with Wan long person, not bear, then grant Wan tell return, yet make Zhi Du govern catch Li shi. Already, shang establish Jiao Dong wang become crown prince, summon Wan, appoint become crown prince tai fu. Long time, move become yu shi da fu. Five years, replace Tao hou She become cheng xiang, court memorial affairs like duty place memorial. Yet self begin official reach cheng xiang, end no able speak. Tian zi with him dun thick, able xiang little jun, honor favor him, reward grant very many.

sentences.zh: 為丞相三歲，景帝崩，武帝立。

translations.literal: Served as Chancellor three years, Emperor Jing passed away, Emperor Wu ascended.
translations.idiomatic: After three years as Chancellor, Emperor Jing died and Emperor Wu ascended the throne.

sentences.zh: 建元年中，丞相以景帝疾時諸官囚多坐不辜者，而君不任職，免之。

translations.literal: In the middle of the first year of Jianyuan, the Chancellor, because during Emperor Jing's illness various official prisoners many sat not guilty, yet he did not bear his duty, dismissed him.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Jianyuan era, the Chancellor was dismissed on the grounds that during Emperor Jing's illness many imprisoned officials had been wrongly convicted, and he had failed to fulfill his duties.

sentences.zh: 其後綰卒，子信代。

translations.literal: Afterward Wan died, son Xin succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Wan died, and his son Xin succeeded to the title.

sentences.zh: 坐酎金失侯。

translations.literal: Convicted for brew gold lost marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: He was convicted of providing impure ritual gold and lost his marquisate.

translations.literal: Become cheng xiang three years, Jing Di collapse, Wu Di establish. Jian Yuan one years middle, cheng xiang with Jing Di illness time various official prisoner many sit not guilty, yet jun not bear duty, free him. Its after Wan die, son Xin replace. Sit brew gold lose hou.

sentences.zh: 塞侯直不疑者，南陽人也。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Sai Zhi Buyi was a person from Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Sai, Zhi Buyi, was a man of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 為郎，事文帝。

translations.literal: Became mounted attendant, served Emperor Wen.
translations.idiomatic: He served as a mounted attendant under Emperor Wen.

sentences.zh: 其同舍有告歸，誤持同舍郎金去，已而金主覺，妄意不疑，不疑謝有之，買金償。

translations.literal: His same quarters had someone returning home, mistakenly took the same quarters attendant's gold and left, afterward the gold owner became aware, wildly suspected Buyi, Buyi apologized as having it, bought gold to repay.
translations.idiomatic: One of his barracks-mates went home on leave and mistakenly took the gold of another attendant in the same quarters. When the owner discovered the loss, he rashly suspected Buyi. Buyi accepted the blame, purchased gold, and repaid him.

sentences.zh: 而告歸者來而歸金，而前郎亡金者大慚，以此稱為長者。

translations.literal: Yet the one returning came and returned the gold, while the previous attendant who lost gold was greatly ashamed, thus called a venerable elder.
translations.idiomatic: Later, the man on leave returned and gave back the gold. The attendant who had lost it was deeply ashamed. For this, Buyi was acclaimed a man of integrity.

sentences.zh: 文帝稱舉，稍遷至太中大夫。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen praised and promoted, gradually moved to Grand Palace Counselor.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen praised and promoted him, and he gradually rose to the rank of Grand Palace Counselor.

sentences.zh: 朝廷見，人或毀曰：「不疑狀貌甚美，然獨無柰其善盜嫂何也！」

translations.literal: Court see, person or destroy said: 'Bu Yi shape appearance very beautiful, yet alone no handle his good thief sister-in-law what?'
translations.idiomatic: In court audiences, people sometimes slandered saying: "Buyi's appearance is very beautiful, yet alone how can he handle his good at stealing sister-in-law!"

sentences.zh: 不疑聞，曰：「我乃無兄。」

translations.literal: Bu Yi hear, said: 'I then no brother.'
translations.idiomatic: Buyi heard, said: "I then have no brother."

sentences.zh: 然終不自明也。

translations.literal: Yet ultimately did not explain himself.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he never once defended himself.

translations.literal: Sai hou Zhi Bu Yi, Nan Yang person. Become lang, serve Wen Di. His same shed have tell return, mistake hold same shed lang gold go, already yet gold master aware, wild intend Bu Yi, Bu Yi thank have it, buy gold repay. Yet tell return come yet return gold, yet front lang lose gold great shame, with this call become long person. Wen Di praise raise, gradually move reach tai zhong da fu. Court see, person or destroy said: 'Bu Yi shape appearance very beautiful, yet alone no handle his good thief sister-in-law what?' Bu Yi hear, said: 'I then no brother.' Yet end not self explain.

sentences.zh: 吳楚反時，不疑以二千石將兵擊之。

translations.literal: When Wu and Chu rebelled, Buyi with two thousand dan led troops to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu and Chu rebelled, Buyi led troops against them in his capacity as a two-thousand-bushel official.

sentences.zh: 景帝後元年，拜為御史大夫。

translations.literal: In the first year of Emperor Jing's reign after Emperor Wen's death, appointed as Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Emperor Jing's latter reign, he was appointed Imperial Secretary.

sentences.zh: 天子修吳楚時功，乃封不疑為塞侯。

translations.literal: The emperor repaired the merits of Wu Chu time, then sealed Buyi as Marquis of Sai.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor reviewed the merits from the Wu and Chu campaign and enfeoffed Buyi as Marquis of Sai.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元年中，與丞相綰俱以過免。

translations.literal: In the middle of the first year of Emperor Wu's Jianyuan, together with Chancellor Wan both dismissed due to faults.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Emperor Wu's Jianyuan era, he was dismissed along with Chancellor Wan on account of their offenses.

translations.literal: Wu Chu rebel time, Bu Yi with two thousand dan lead troops strike it. Jing Di after one year, appoint become yu shi da fu. Tian zi repair Wu Chu time merit, then seal Bu Yi become Sai hou. Wu Di Jian Yuan one years middle, with cheng xiang Wan both with fault free.

sentences.zh: 不疑學老子言。

translations.literal: Buyi studied Laozi's teachings.
translations.idiomatic: Buyi studied the teachings of Laozi.

sentences.zh: 其所臨，為官如故，唯恐人知其為吏跡也。

translations.literal: Wherever he was assigned, served as official like before, only fearing people know his official traces.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever he was posted, he served in office as before, fearing only that others might discern his traces as an official.

sentences.zh: 不好立名稱，稱為長者。

translations.literal: Not fond of establishing name and reputation, called a venerable elder.
translations.idiomatic: He had no fondness for building a name or reputation, and so was called a man of integrity.

sentences.zh: 不疑卒，子相如代。

translations.literal: Buyi died, son Xiangru succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Buyi died, his son Xiangru succeeded to the title.

sentences.zh: 孫望，坐酎金失侯。

translations.literal: Grandson Wang convicted for brew gold lost marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: His grandson Wang was convicted of providing impure ritual gold and lost the marquisate.

translations.literal: Bu Yi study Lao Zi speak. His place arrive, become official like old, only fear person know his become li trace. Not good establish name claim, call become long person. Bu Yi die, son Xiang Ru replace. Grandson Wang, sit brew gold lose hou.

sentences.zh: 郎中令周文者，名仁，其先故任城人也。

translations.literal: The Palace Steward Zhou Wen, named Ren, his ancestors were formerly from Rencheng.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace Steward Zhou Wen, whose given name was Ren, had ancestors originally from Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 以醫見。

translations.literal: Appeared through medicine.
translations.idiomatic: He first came to notice through his skill in medicine.

sentences.zh: 景帝為太子時，拜為舍人，積功稍遷，孝文帝時至太中大夫。

translations.literal: When Emperor Jing was crown prince, appointed as retainer, accumulating merit gradually promoted, during Emperor Wen's time reached Grand Palace Counselor.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing was still Crown Prince, Ren was appointed as a retainer. Through accumulated merit he was gradually promoted, reaching the rank of Grand Palace Counselor by Emperor Wen's time.

sentences.zh: 景帝初即位，拜仁為郎中令。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing first ascended, appointed Ren as Palace Steward.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing first ascended the throne, he appointed Ren as Palace Steward.

translations.literal: Lang zhong ling Zhou Wen, name Ren, his first former Ren Cheng person. With medicine see. Jing Di become crown prince time, appoint become she ren, accumulate merit gradually move, Xiao Wen Di time reach tai zhong da fu. Jing Di begin establish, appoint Ren become lang zhong ling.

sentences.zh: 仁為人陰重不泄，常衣敝補衣溺袴，期為不絜清，以是得幸。

translations.literal: Ren as person was deep and heavy not leaking, constantly wore worn patched clothes urine pants, pretended to be not clean and pure, thus obtained favor.
translations.idiomatic: Ren was by nature secretive and discreet, never divulging anything. He habitually wore worn and patched clothes and soiled trousers, deliberately making himself appear unkempt, and thus won the emperor's favor.

sentences.zh: 景帝入臥內，於後宮祕戲，仁常在旁。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing entered sleeping quarters, in the rear palace secret games, Ren constantly at side.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing retired to his private chambers and engaged in secret amusements in the rear palace, Ren was always at his side.

sentences.zh: 至景帝崩，仁尚為郎中令，終無所言。

translations.literal: Until Emperor Jing passed away, Ren still served as Palace Steward, ultimately had nothing to say.
translations.idiomatic: Until Emperor Jing's death, Ren still served as Palace Steward, and to the end he never uttered a word about what he had seen.

sentences.zh: 上時問人，仁曰：「上自察之。」

translations.literal: Shang time ask person, Ren said: 'Shang self examine it.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sometimes asked people, Ren said: "Your Majesty examine it yourself."

sentences.zh: 然亦無所毀。

translations.literal: Yet also had nothing to slander.
translations.idiomatic: Yet neither did he ever slander anyone.

sentences.zh: 以此景帝再自幸其家。

translations.literal: With this Emperor Jing twice personally visited his home.
translations.idiomatic: For this reason, Emperor Jing twice personally visited his home.

sentences.zh: 家徙陽陵。

translations.literal: The home moved to Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: His family relocated to Yangling.

sentences.zh: 上所賜甚多，然常讓，不敢受也。

translations.literal: What the emperor granted was very many, yet he constantly yielded, not daring to receive.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's gifts to him were many, yet he always declined, not daring to accept.

sentences.zh: 諸侯群臣賂遺，終無所受。

translations.literal: Feudal lords and assembled ministers' gifts and remembrances, ultimately received none.
translations.idiomatic: Gifts and offerings from feudal lords and ministers he would never accept.

translations.literal: Ren become person yin heavy not leak, constant wear old patch clothes urine pants, period become not jie clear, with this obtain fortunate. Jing Di enter sleep inner, at back palace secret play, Ren constant at side. Reach Jing Di collapse, Ren still become lang zhong ling, end no place speak. Shang time ask person, Ren said: 'Shang self examine it.' Yet also no place destroy. With this Jing Di again self fortunate his home. Home move Yang Ling. Shang place grant very many, yet constant yield, not dare receive. Various hou group minister lu yi, end no place receive.

sentences.zh: 武帝立，以為先帝臣，重之。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu ascended, considered him former emperor's minister, honored him.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu ascended the throne, he regarded Ren as a minister of the former emperor and treated him with great respect.

sentences.zh: 仁乃病免，以二千石祿歸老，子孫咸至大官矣。

translations.literal: Ren then fell ill and was dismissed, with two thousand dan salary returned to retire, sons and grandsons all reached high office.
translations.idiomatic: Ren eventually fell ill and was relieved of his duties, retiring on a salary of two thousand bushels. His sons and grandsons all rose to high office.

translations.literal: Wu Di establish, with become former di minister, heavy him. Ren then illness free, with two thousand dan lu return old, sons grandson all reach big official.

sentences.zh: 御史大夫張叔者，名歐，安丘侯說之庶子也。

translations.literal: The Imperial Secretary Zhang Shu, named Ou, was the illegitimate son of the Marquis of Anqiu, Yue.
translations.idiomatic: The Imperial Secretary Zhang Shu, whose given name was Ou, was a concubine's son of the Marquis of Anqiu, Yue.

sentences.zh: 孝文時以治刑名言事太子。

translations.literal: During Emperor Wen's time, through governing penal codes spoke affairs to the crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: During Emperor Wen's time, he presented affairs to the Crown Prince through his expertise in the Legalist school of penal law and terminology.

sentences.zh: 然歐雖治刑名家，其人長者。

translations.literal: Yet though Ou governed penal codes school, his person was a venerable elder.
translations.idiomatic: Yet although Ou was a practitioner of the Legalist school, he was himself a man of integrity.

sentences.zh: 景帝時尊重，常為九卿。

translations.literal: During Emperor Jing's time respected and honored, constantly became one of the Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: During Emperor Jing's reign he was respected and honored, regularly serving as one of the Nine Ministers.

sentences.zh: 至武帝元朔四年，韓安國免，詔拜歐為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Reached Emperor Wu's Yuanshuo fourth year, Han Anguo dismissed, commanded to appoint Ou as Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Emperor Wu's Yuanshuo era, Han Anguo was dismissed, and an edict was issued appointing Ou as Imperial Secretary.

sentences.zh: 自歐為吏，未嘗言案人，專以誠長者處官。

translations.literal: Since Ou became official, never once spoke of prosecuting people, specialized in sincerely as venerable elder administering office.
translations.idiomatic: From the time Ou first became an official, he never once spoke of prosecuting anyone, devoting himself entirely to administering his office with sincerity and the manner of a man of integrity.

sentences.zh: 官屬以為長者，亦不敢大欺。

translations.literal: Subordinates considered him venerable elder, also dared not greatly deceive.
translations.idiomatic: His subordinates regarded him as a man of integrity and did not dare to deceive him greatly.

sentences.zh: 上具獄事，有可卻，卻之；

translations.literal: Shang prepare prison affairs, have able reject, reject it;
translations.idiomatic: The emperor prepared prison affairs, if could reject, rejected them;

sentences.zh: 不可者，不得已，為涕泣面對而封之。

translations.literal: If not able, unavoidably, became tearful weeping face to face and sealed them.
translations.idiomatic: When he could not reject them, he had no choice but to weep as he sealed the verdicts face to face.

sentences.zh: 其愛人如此。

translations.literal: His loving people was like this.
translations.idiomatic: Such was his compassion for others.

translations.literal: Yu shi da fu Zhang Shu, name Ou, An Qiu hou Shuo common son. Xiao Wen time with govern punishment name speak affairs crown prince. Yet Ou though govern punishment name home, his person long person. Jing Di time respect heavy, constant become nine qing. Reach Wu Di Yuan Shuo four years, Han An Guo free, zhao appoint Ou become yu shi da fu. Self Ou become li, not once speak case person, specialize with sincere long person place official. Official belong with him long person, also not dare big cheat. Shang prepare prison affairs, have able reject, reject it; Not able, not get already, become cry weep face face yet seal it. His love person like this.

sentences.zh: 老病甐，請免。

translations.literal: Old and ill with dropsy, requested dismissal.
translations.idiomatic: When he grew old and fell ill with dropsy, he requested to be relieved of his duties.

sentences.zh: 於是天子亦策罷，以上大夫祿歸老于家。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu said: "How is the old man?"
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon the emperor also issued an edict relieving him of office, and he retired to his home on the salary of a Senior Grand Counselor.

sentences.zh: 家於陽陵。

translations.literal: Made home at Yangling.

sentences.zh: 子孫咸至大官矣。

translations.literal: Sons and grandsons all reached high office.
Old illness pi, please free. Then tian zi also strategy stop, with shang da fu lu return old at home. Home at Yang Ling. Sons grandson all reach big official.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：仲尼有言曰「君子欲訥於言而敏於行」，其萬石、建陵、張叔之謂邪？

translations.literal: Tai shi gong said: Zhong Ni have speak said 'jun zi desire ne at speak yet min at conduct', its Wan Shi, Jian Ling, Zhang Shu wait call?
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian says: Confucius had words saying "The gentleman desires to be hesitant in speech but diligent in action," is this not what Wan Shi, Jianling, Zhang Shu and the like are called?

sentences.zh: 是以其教不肅而成，不嚴而治。

translations.literal: Therefore their teaching was not stern yet accomplished, not strict yet governed.

sentences.zh: 塞侯微巧，而周文處讇君子譏之，為其近於佞也。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Sai was slightly skillful, yet Zhou Wen resided in flattery—the gentlemen censured him, because he approached sycophancy.

sentences.zh: 然斯可謂篤行君子矣！

translations.literal: Yet si able call du conduct jun zi!
translations.idiomatic: Yet these can be called steadfast gentlemen!

translations.literal: Tai shi gong said: Zhong Ni have speak said 'jun zi desire ne at speak yet min at conduct', its Wan Shi, Jian Ling, Zhang Shu wait call? Therefore their teach not su yet become, not strict yet govern. Sai hou wei qiao, yet Zhou Wen place chan jun zi ji him, become his near at ning. Yet si able call du conduct jun zi!
